title: Promise Workers, Backpressure, Abort Propagation, and Clean Drain · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded abortable worker pipeline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence de…
objectives: Admit work through page, origin, byte, memory, browser, file, and report budgets before creating expensive Promises.
Classify per-item failure without losing sibling settlements, aggregate cause, or crawl-level stop conditions.
Propagate capacity from report and storage sinks through parsing, fetch, frontier, and discovery instead of buffering without bound.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Work Admission
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 2838 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove multiresource admission, rejection isolation, backpressure, abort propagation, and handle-clean drain; owner authorization and revocation must remain separate from robots and legal-review claims; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Admit work through page, origin, byte, memory, browser, file, and report budgets before creating expensive Promises.

Inspect the Work Admission trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Work Admission.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for admit work through page, origin, byte, memory, browser, file, and report budgets before creating expensive promises. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Work Admission, keep Work Admission connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.instruction:
